title: Natural fabric softener
subtitle: A white vinegar rinse with lavender and rose geranium for the final wash cycle
excerpt: White vinegar added to the fabric-softener drawer neutralises detergent residue left in fibres, leaving laundry softer and reducing static. The vinegar smell fully disappears in the dryer or on the line. Lavender and rose geranium add a faint lasting scent.
sourceNotes: Acetic acid neutralisation of detergent alkalinity in rinse water: standard textile chemistry. White vinegar as a fabric softener alternative: widely documented in household chemistry literature.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 500 ml bottle of fabric softener
recipe.batchNotes: Make in large batches -- this is a rinse product used at a high dilution, so a 500 ml bottle lasts many washes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: White vinegar keeps indefinitely. Make the scented version in batches of 500 ml and replace when the essential oil scent has faded.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keeps at least a year at room temperature. Discard if the bottle develops a sediment or unusual smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: 5% acetic-acid spirit vinegar. In laundry it neutralises alkaline detergent residue in fibres, softening fabric without chemical additives. The smell disappears on drying.

content.text: Commercial fabric softeners coat fibres with a waxy film that makes them feel soft. This recipe works differently:

neutralises the alkaline detergent residue left in cloth fibres after washing, which is what causes the stiff, scratchy feel. It also reduces static cling and mineral build-up from hard water. The vinegar smell disappears completely when the laundry dries.

Pour 500 ml of white vinegar into a clean bottle or jar. Add the essential oils if using and swirl to combine. Essential oils float rather than dissolve -- the bottle will need a quick shake before each use.

Add 60 to 100 ml (about 4 to 6 tablespoons) to the fabric-softener compartment of your washing machine before running the cycle. For front-loaders with a dispenser drawer, pour it into the softener slot. For top-loaders without a softener compartment, add it during the final rinse.

Safe to use in all standard domestic washing machines. The diluted acetic acid poses no risk to seals or drum linings. Do not pour undiluted vinegar directly onto the drum gasket seal -- add it to the softener compartment only. Long-term use can affect rubber door seals on some older machines; if you notice the seal softening, reduce frequency.

Keep at room temperature in a sealed bottle. Vinegar does not spoil. The essential oil scent will fade after about a year as the oils oxidise. Discard if the bottle develops a sediment or an off smell.
